notes: Grounding is live web search against real boards/labs; no fixture KB. The hard part is currency and honesty: drop expired/filled listings, stamp verified_on, never invent a deadline. Assertion kinds: file = a written opening record; chat = the assistant's reply; schema = output passes shared/schemas/validate_kb.py.

evals.name: funded-gnn-europe-currency
evals.prompt: Find me fully-funded PhD positions on graph neural networks or geometric deep learning in the UK or Europe, starting 2027. I need funding.
evals.expected_output: Several schema-conformant opening records in knowledge-base/openings/, each with funding set explicitly and verified_on stamped to today. Honors the funding requirement (excludes/flags self-funded), respects the 2027 start year (distinguishes 2026 from 2027 cycles), and flags any listing it could not verify rather than fabricating a deadline.

assertions.text: Produces >=3 per-opening schema files in knowledge-base/openings/
Each opening file passes shared/schemas/validate_kb.py (funding/status/dates valid)
Sets verified_on to today on each recorded opening
Honors the funding requirement: excludes or flags self-funded positions
Respects the 2027 start-year filter; does not pass off a 2026 cycle as 2027
Flags unverified/expired listings or leaves unknown fields blank rather than fabricating

evals.name: topic-to-labs-us-recruiting
evals.prompt: I'm interested in mechanistic interpretability of large language models. Which US labs or professors are recruiting PhD students in this area? Find me some openings to target.
evals.expected_output: Identifies US labs with an explicit recruiting signal (treats 'this lab is plausibly recruiting' as a valid opening with funding: unknown and a note on the admission model), writes per-opening records each saying why it fits, and notes the US program-application route. No fabricated 'positions'.

assertions.text: Identifies labs with an explicit recruiting signal rather than inventing postings
Records funding: unknown and notes the US admission model for direct-to-advisor cases
Each opening states the concrete topical fit, not 'matches your interests'

evals.name: field-agnostic-expired-discipline
evals.prompt: I'm a molecular biology MSc looking for funded PhD positions on RNA therapeutics in Europe for a 2027 start. A lot of what I find online seems to be old calls — please only give me ones that are actually open.
evals.expected_output: Demonstrates field-agnosticism (life-sciences boards/sources, not arXiv) AND currency discipline: follows each hit to its primary page, drops calls whose deadline has passed or whose cycle is over, stamps verified_on on the survivors, and is explicit about how few genuinely-open 2027 listings exist this early rather than padding with stale ones.

assertions.text: Uses life-sciences-appropriate sources (e.g. EURAXESS, FindAPhD, dept pages), not arXiv
Drops or clearly flags expired / past-deadline listings instead of presenting them as open
Stamps verified_on on each recorded opening
Honest about scarcity of genuine 2027 listings rather than fabricating to hit a count
